truth.papers.A38.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel manufactured by selective laser melting
truth.papers.A38.paper_scope: This study investigates the influence of laser power on the microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel fabricated by selective laser melting for the first time.

prediction.papers.A38.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel manufactured by selective laser melting
prediction.papers.A38.paper_scope: First-time fabrication of Fe-30Mn-9Al-C-3Ni low-density steel by selective laser melting (SLM) using PREP-prepared powder, systematically investigating the influence of laser power on microstructure and mechanical properties.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9alc3ni.alloys_notes: Powder chemical composition determined by inductively coupled plasma (ICP-OES). PREP-prepared powder with spherical morphology, particle size 15-53 μm, D50=35.69 μm, Hall flow 14 s/50 g.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9alc3ni.alloys_notes: Spherical PREP-prepared powder with particle diameter 15–53 μm, D50 = 35.69 μm, Hall flowability 14 s/50 g, low oxygen content, and solid particles without hollow structures.

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9alc3ni_70W.descrip: Selective laser melting (SLM) at laser power 70 W, energy density 58.33 J/mm³, scanning speed 750 mm/s, spot diameter 0.1 mm, layer thickness 0.02 mm, hatch space 0.08 mm, substrate preheated to 200 °C, argon atmosphere with oxygen < 300 ppm, 67° rotation angle per layer.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9alc3ni_90W.descrip: Selective laser melting (SLM) at laser power 90 W, energy density 75.00 J/mm³, other parameters same as 70 W process (scanning speed 750 mm/s, spot 0.1 mm, layer 0.02 mm, hatch 0.08 mm, substrate 200 °C, Ar shield).
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9alc3ni_110W.descri: Selective laser melting (SLM) at laser power 110 W, energy density 91.67 J/mm³, other parameters same as 70 W process.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9alc3ni_130W.descri: Selective laser melting (SLM) at laser power 130 W, energy density 108.33 J/mm³, other parameters same as 70 W process.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9alc3ni_150W.descri: Selective laser melting (SLM) at laser power 150 W, energy density 125.00 J/mm³, other parameters same as 70 W process.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9alc3ni_as-cast.des: As-cast condition (no further processing details provided).

truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_70W.over: Predominantly austenite phase with nano-sized κ carbide, containing 4.75% porosity, some unfused powder particles and microcracks. Equiaxed grains with random orientations, average grain size 8.1 μm.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_90W.over: Austenite with nano κ carbide, 0.25% porosity, mostly fusion with few defects. Equiaxed grains, average size 10.6 μm.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_110W.ove: Austenite with nano κ carbide, lowest porosity 0.13%, highest densification. Equiaxed grains in transverse section (avg 11.0 μm), columnar grains in longitudinal section (avg 14.9 μm). Dislocation tangles present.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_110W.mic: random (transverse), columnar growth along build direction
uniformly distributed in austenite matrix; orientation relationship [011]γ//[011]κ, dark-field image shows nano-particles.
oriented with austenite, SADP consistent with [011]γ+κ
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_130W.ove: Austenite with κ carbide, porosity 0.28%, average grain size 13.9 μm. Some spheroidization and small spheres observed on surface.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_150W.ove: Austenite with κ carbide, porosity 0.35%, cracks present. Average grain size 14.3 μm. Surface exhibits spheroidization and internal cracks.

prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_70W.over: Austenite matrix with nano-sized κ carbide precipitates and equiaxed grains
prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_90W.over: Austenite matrix with nano-sized κ carbide precipitates and equiaxed grains
prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_110W.ove: Austenite matrix with nano-sized κ carbide; equiaxed grains in cross-section and columnar grains in longitudinal section
prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_130W.ove: Austenite matrix with nano-sized κ carbide precipitates and equiaxed grains
prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_150W.ove: Austenite matrix with nano-sized κ carbide precipitates and equiaxed grains

truth.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9alc3ni_110W.pha: oriented: [011]γ // [011]κ, SADP reveals coherent or semi-coherent relationship
κ carbide precipitates during SLM process as nano-particles uniformly distributed in austenite matrix.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn9a: Optical microscopy (Leica DMi8) and image analysis
Porosity measurement and melt pool observation
Porosity first decreases then increases with power: 4.75% (P-70), 0.25% (P-90), 0.13% (P-110), 0.28% (P-130), 0.35% (P-150). Corresponding relative densities given.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn9a: Yield strength 776.9 MPa, tensile strength 986.4 MPa, elongation 23.10%; hardness 278 HV (cross-section) and 279 HV (longitudinal section).
Yield strength 802.4 MPa, tensile strength 1067.8 MPa, elongation 39.68%; hardness 285 HV (cross-section) and 289 HV (longitudinal section).

Yield strength 887.1 MPa, tensile strength 1076.8 MPa, elongation 40.80%; hardness 292 HV (cross-section) and 294 HV (longitudinal section); best comprehensive mechanical properties among all samples.
Yield strength 782.2 MPa, tensile strength 1039.6 MPa, elongation 39.72%; hardness 283 HV (cross-section) and 284 HV (longitudinal section).
Yield strength 771.5 MPa, tensile strength 1032.0 MPa, elongation 28.28%; hardness 281 HV (cross-section) and 283 HV (longitudinal section).
Yield strength 772.3 MPa, tensile strength 879.0 MPa, elongation 20.53%; lower strength and plasticity than SLM samples due to coarse grains.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni powder was prepared by plasma rotating electrode process (PREP); powder is highly spherical, no satellites, narrow size distribution 15-53 μm, D50=35.69 μm, Hall flow 14 s/50 g, low oxygen (0.01 wt%).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The minimum density among the SLM samples is 6.78 g/cm³, which is much lighter than 304 stainless steel (7.93 g/cm³).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Surface roughness decreases with increasing laser power: P-70 20.71 μm, P-90 9.19 μm, P-110 5.29 μm, P-130 4.65 μm, P-150 4.19 μm (measured by laser confocal microscopy).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Laser energy densities: 58.33, 75.00, 91.67, 108.33, 125.00 J/mm³ for 70-150 W, respectively.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Scanning strategy: overlapping strips with 67° rotation angle per layer to reduce crystallographic texture.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: XRD of powder shows both γ-Fe(M) and α-Fe(M) phases; SLM suppresses α-Fe formation due to fast cooling.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: TEM of P-110: interplanar spacings (200) 0.262 nm, (1-11) 0.223 nm; fast Fourier transform confirms [011] austenite zone axis.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: As-cast sample was used for comparison; its processing history is not detailed.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Fracture surfaces: P-70 shows decohesion and internal cracks; P-130 and P-150 show spheroidization spheres and cleavage facets, reducing ductility.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: The study is the first to apply SLM to Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: PREP-prepared Fe-30Mn-9Al-C-3Ni powder has a median particle size D50 of 35.69 μm, Hall flowability of 14 s/50 g, and solid particles without hollow structures.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The minimum density among SLM-printed samples is 6.78 g/cm³, which is much lower than that of 304 stainless steel (7.93 g/cm³).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: FWHM values of the (111) diffraction peaks for P-70, P-90, P-110, P-130, and P-150 are 0.289, 0.274, 0.263, 0.255, and 0.237 respectively, indicating increasing grain size with increasing laser energy density.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Tensile fracture surfaces: P-70 shows lack of fusion and internal cracks; P-90 and P-110 exhibit dominant ductile dimples; P-130 and P-150 show internal pores, cracks, and balling leading to reduced plasticity.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: XRD pattern of PREP powder shows diffraction peaks for both γ-Fe(M) and α-Fe(M) phases, while SLM-printed samples contain only austenite γ-Fe(M) due to rapid cooling suppressing ferrite formation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Detailed grain-size distributions for all SLM samples are provided in Fig. 8; grain size continuously increases from 8.1 μm (P-70) to 14.3 μm (P-150) in the cross-section.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The side surface of SLM-printed samples was observed to be softer than the top surface in hardness measurements.
